title: contingency-dsl Composed — Two-Process Theory (Theoretical Meta-Schema)
description: Meta-schema marking a phase or phase-sequence as interpreted under the Rescorla-Solomon (1967) two-process framework. Two-process theory is the unifying theoretical account rather than a procedure in its own right; this schema therefore provides a lightweight theory tag that downstream analysis tooling can consume. It does NOT define a new contingency structure — CER, PIT, autoshape, and omission remain the concrete composed procedures.

TwoProcessTheoryTag.description: A non-structural marker that the enclosing phase (or phase sequence) is analyzed under two-process theory. The tag carries optional references to the composed sub-procedures present, which helps downstream tooling cross-link CER / PIT / autoshape / omission interpretations to the theoretical framework.

framework.description: Identifies the specific two-process framework. Reserved as a const for future extensibility (e.g., Mowrer's earlier two-factor account).

composed_constituents.description: Advisory list of composed procedures present in the enclosing context. Purely informational; Phase 2 validation does not enforce consistency.

interpretive_claims.description: Optional enumeration of the four interlocking propositions of two-process theory that the program's analysis relies on. Used by reporting tools to generate claim-specific output sections.
